description: A security-focused API key broker designed to eliminate plaintext provider secrets in application environments using a split-trust architecture.

architecture.encryption: V3 Asymmetric Envelope Encryption (RSA-4096 hybrid with AES-256-GCM)
architecture.binding: AAD (Associated Authenticated Data) binding to key_id and policy_hash

trust_boundary.remote: Cloudflare Worker + SubumbraVault Durable Object (holds RSA private key)

core_services.name: subumbra-proxy
core_services.role: Transparent proxy for applications; validates adapter tokens; coordinates with Cloudflare.

core_services.name: subumbra-keys
core_services.role: Encrypted record store and audit logger; manages V3 envelopes in SQLite.

core_services.name: Cloudflare Worker
core_services.role: The decryption authority; enforces policies; performs upstream provider calls.

security_invariants: No plaintext provider secrets are stored on the local host.
Decryption authority is separated from encrypted storage.
Policy changes on the host break the cryptographic seal (AAD) of the key.
Provider keys exist only in Cloudflare Worker memory during active requests.
